claim.statement: A symptom-tracking system should treat short-term improvement as a proxy signal until durable functional outcomes are verified.
claim.mechanistic_basis: Short-term changes can reflect noise, regression to the mean, context changes, placebo-like effects, measurement artifacts, or temporary compensation rather than mechanism-level repair.
claim.scope_boundary: This packet defines research-memory routing only and does not recommend diagnosis, treatment, or clinical action.

proxy_signals.name: daily_symptom_score_change
proxy_signals.measurement: Self-reported daily severity score over seven days.
proxy_signals.why_only_proxy: A short time window can move independently of durable function or verified disease modification.
proxy_signals.failure_mode: The system may over-promote a transient improvement as evidence of cure.

target_endpoints.name: durable_functional_stability
target_endpoints.measurement: Sustained function across repeated contexts with stable or improved objective and subjective markers.
target_endpoints.promotion_threshold: Improvement persists across context changes and survives confounder review.

time_horizon.reason_endpoint_needs_longer_window: Durability, recurrence, confounding, and adaptation cannot be distinguished from a brief observation window.

validation_plan.dataset_boundary: Synthetic deidentified observations only; no raw private health or animal-care logs in this fixture.
validation_plan.comparison_logic: Compare proxy movement against later endpoint persistence and confounder-adjusted context changes.

packet.falsification_route: Reject or hold the claim if proxy improvement does not persist into the endpoint window or if confounders explain the change better than the proposed mechanism.

claim.statement: Three better days proves the intervention cured the condition.
